# google_compute_ha_controller

Represents an HA Controller resource.



<div class = "oics-button" style="float: right; margin: 0 0 -15px">
  <a href="https://console.cloud.google.com/cloudshell/open?cloudshell_git_repo=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fterraform-google-modules%2Fdocs-examples.git&cloudshell_image=gcr.io%2Fcloudshell-images%2Fcloudshell%3Alatest&cloudshell_print=.%2Fmotd&cloudshell_tutorial=.%2Ftutorial.md&cloudshell_working_dir=compute_ha_controller_basic&open_in_editor=main.tf" target="_blank">
    <img alt="Open in Cloud Shell" src="//gstatic.com/cloudssh/images/open-btn.svg" style="max-height: 44px; margin: 32px auto; max-width: 100%;">
  </a>
</div>
## Example Usage - Compute Ha Controller Basic


```hcl
locals {
  region          = "us-central1"
  zone1           = "us-central1-a"
  zone2           = "us-central1-b"
}

data "google_compute_image" "debian_12" {
  provider = google-private
  family   = "debian-12"
  project  = "debian-cloud"
}

resource "google_compute_region_disk" "regional_disk" {
  provider                  = google-private
  name                      = "hac-bs-rd"
  physical_block_size_bytes = 4096
  type                      = "pd-balanced"
  region                    = local.region
  replica_zones             = [local.zone1, local.zone2]
  size                      = 10
  image                     = data.google_compute_image.debian_12.self_link
}

resource "google_compute_instance" "vm" {
  provider     = google-private
  name         = "hac-bs-inst"
  machine_type = "e2-micro"
  zone         = local.zone1

  boot_disk {
    auto_delete = false
    source      = google_compute_region_disk.regional_disk.self_link
  }

  network_interface {
    network = "default"
  }

  allow_stopping_for_update = true
}

resource "google_compute_ha_controller" "default" {
  provider            = google-private
  name                = "hac-bs"
  region              = local.region
  instance_name       = google_compute_instance.vm.name
  failover_initiation = "MANUAL_ONLY"

  zone_configuration {
    zone = local.zone1
  }
  zone_configuration {
    zone = local.zone2
  }
}
```

## Example Usage - Compute Ha Controller Reservations


```hcl
locals {
  region          = "us-central1"
  zone1           = "us-central1-a"
  zone2           = "us-central1-b"
}

data "google_compute_image" "debian_12" {
  provider = google-private
  family   = "debian-12"
  project  = "debian-cloud"
}

resource "google_compute_region_disk" "regional_disk" {
  provider                  = google-private
  name                      = "hac-rv-rd"
  physical_block_size_bytes = 4096
  type                      = "pd-balanced"
  region                    = local.region
  replica_zones             = [local.zone1, local.zone2]
  size                      = 10
  image                     = data.google_compute_image.debian_12.self_link
}

resource "google_compute_reservation" "reservation_zone1" {
  provider = google-private
  name     = "hac-rv-rv" # same name as in zone 2
  zone     = local.zone1

  specific_reservation {
    count = 1
    instance_properties {
      machine_type = "e2-micro"
    }
  }

  reservation_sharing_policy {
    service_share_type = "DISALLOW_ALL"
  }

  specific_reservation_required = true
}

resource "google_compute_reservation" "reservation_zone2" {
  provider = google-private
  name     = "hac-rv-rv" # same name as in zone 1
  zone     = local.zone2

  specific_reservation {
    count = 1
    instance_properties {
      machine_type = "e2-micro"
    }
  }

  reservation_sharing_policy {
    service_share_type = "DISALLOW_ALL"
  }

  specific_reservation_required = true
}

resource "google_compute_instance" "vm" {
  provider     = google-private
  name         = "hac-rv-inst"
  machine_type = "e2-micro"
  zone         = local.zone1

  boot_disk {
    auto_delete = false
    source      = google_compute_region_disk.regional_disk.self_link
  }

  network_interface {
    subnetwork = "default"
    stack_type = "IPV4_ONLY"
  }

  reservation_affinity {
    type = "SPECIFIC_RESERVATION"
    specific_reservation {
      key    = "compute.googleapis.com/reservation-name"
      values = [google_compute_reservation.reservation_zone1.name]
    }
  }

  allow_stopping_for_update = true
}

resource "google_compute_ha_controller" "default" {
  provider            = google-private
  name                = "hac-rv"
  region              = local.region
  instance_name       = google_compute_instance.vm.name
  failover_initiation = "MANUAL_ONLY"

  networking_auto_configuration {
    internal {
      stack_type = "IPV4_ONLY"
    }
  }

  zone_configuration {
    zone = local.zone1
    reservation_affinity {
      type = "SPECIFIC_RESERVATION"
      specific_reservation {
        key    = "compute.googleapis.com/reservation-name"
        values = [google_compute_reservation.reservation_zone1.name]
      }
    }
  }
  zone_configuration {
    zone = local.zone2
    reservation_affinity {
      type = "SPECIFIC_RESERVATION"
      specific_reservation {
        key    = "compute.googleapis.com/reservation-name"
        values = [google_compute_reservation.reservation_zone2.name]
      }
    }
  }
}
```
<div class = "oics-button" style="float: right; margin: 0 0 -15px">

## Example Usage - Compute Ha Controller Sole Tenancy


```hcl
locals {
  region          = "us-central1"
  zone1           = "us-central1-a"
  zone2           = "us-central1-b"
}

data "google_compute_image" "debian_12" {
  provider = google-private
  family   = "debian-12"
  project  = "debian-cloud"
}

resource "google_compute_region_disk" "regional_disk" {
  provider                  = google-private
  name                      = "hac-st-rd"
  physical_block_size_bytes = 4096
  type                      = "pd-balanced"
  region                    = local.region
  replica_zones             = [local.zone1, local.zone2]
  size                      = 10
  image                     = data.google_compute_image.debian_12.self_link
}

resource "google_compute_node_template" "node_template1" {
  provider  = google-private
  name      = "hac-st-ntmpl"
  region    = local.region
  node_type = "n1-node-96-624"
  node_affinity_labels = {
    workload = "synthetic"
    distinct = "1"
  }
}

resource "google_compute_node_template" "node_template2" {
  provider  = google-private
  name      = "hac-st-ntmpl2"
  region    = local.region
  node_type = "n1-node-96-624"
  node_affinity_labels = {
    workload = "synthetic"
    distinct = "2"
  }
}

resource "google_compute_node_group" "sole_tenant_group1" {
  provider      = google-private
  name          = "hac-st-stgrp1"
  zone          = local.zone1
  node_template = google_compute_node_template.node_template1.id
  initial_size  = 1
}

resource "google_compute_node_group" "sole_tenant_group2" {
  provider      = google-private
  name          = "hac-st-stgrp2"
  zone          = local.zone2
  node_template = google_compute_node_template.node_template2.id
  initial_size  = 1
}

resource "google_compute_instance" "vm" {
  provider     = google-private
  name         = "hac-st-inst"
  machine_type = "n1-standard-1"
  zone         = local.zone1

  depends_on = [
    google_compute_node_group.sole_tenant_group1,
    google_compute_node_group.sole_tenant_group2,
  ]

  boot_disk {
    auto_delete = false
    source      = google_compute_region_disk.regional_disk.self_link
  }

  network_interface {
    subnetwork = "default"
    stack_type = "IPV4_ONLY"
  }

  scheduling {
    node_affinities {
      key      = "workload"
      operator = "IN"
      values   = ["synthetic"]
    }
  }

  allow_stopping_for_update = true
}

resource "google_compute_ha_controller" "default" {
  provider            = google-private
  name                = "hac-st"
  region              = local.region
  instance_name       = google_compute_instance.vm.name
  failover_initiation = "MANUAL_ONLY"

  zone_configuration {
    zone = local.zone1
    node_affinities {
      key      = "workload"
      operator = "IN"
      values   = ["synthetic"]
    }
  }
  zone_configuration {
    zone = local.zone2
    node_affinities {
      key      = "workload"
      operator = "IN"
      values   = ["synthetic"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:


* `name` -
  (Required)
  Name of the resource.
  The name must be 1-63 characters long, and comply with RFC1035.

* `instance_name` -
  (Required)
  Name of the instance that HaController is in charge of.
  Must comply with RFC1035. Instance must exist in one of two provided zones.

* `zone_configuration` -
  (Required)
  Set of 2 distinct zones configurations.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_zone_configuration).

* `region` -
  (Required)
  The region where the HA Controller will be created.


* `description` -
  (Optional)
  A description of this resource.
  Maximum length of 2048 characters.

* `failover_initiation` -
  (Optional)
  Indicates how failover should be initiated.
  Possible values are:
  - "AUTOMATIC": Automatically detect zonal outages and perform failover.
  - "MANUAL_ONLY": Failover is not initiated automatically, a user has to perform it manually.
  Possible values are: `AUTOMATIC`, `MANUAL_ONLY`.

* `networking_auto_configuration` -
  (Optional)
  Basic networking configuration. Required backend services and
  forwarding rules will be automatically created with default parameters.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_networking_auto_configuration).

* `backend_services` -
  (Optional)
  Currently, only one backend service can be specified,
  and it must be for an L4 Internal Load Balancer (ILB).
  Provide the full URL of the backend service.

* `project` - (Optional) The ID of the project in which the resource belongs.
    If it is not provided, the provider project is used.

* `deletion_policy` - (Optional) Whether Terraform will be prevented from destroying the resource. Defaults to DELETE.
	When a 'terraform destroy' or 'terraform apply' would delete the resource,
	the command will fail if this field is set to "PREVENT" in Terraform state.
	When set to "ABANDON", the command will remove the resource from Terraform
	management without updating or deleting the resource in the API.
	When set to "DELETE", deleting the resource is allowed.


<a name="nested_zone_configuration"></a>The `zone_configuration` block supports:

* `zone` -
  (Required)
  Required, should be unique for every set item.

* `reservation_affinity` -
  (Optional)
  A nested object resource.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_zone_configuration_reservation_affinity).

* `node_affinities` -
  (Optional)
  Specifies node affinities or anti-affinities to determine
  which sole-tenant nodes your instances and managed instance groups
  will use as host systems
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_zone_configuration_node_affinities).


<a name="nested_zone_configuration_reservation_affinity"></a>The `reservation_affinity` block supports:

* `type` -
  (Optional)
  Specifies the type of reservation from which this instance can consume resources. Possible values:
  - "NO_RESERVATION": Do not consume from any allocated capacity.
  - "ANY_RESERVATION": Consume any allocation available.
  - "SPECIFIC_RESERVATION": Must consume from a specific reservation (key and values must be set).
  - "SPECIFIC_THEN_ANY_RESERVATION": Prefer specific, fallback to any.
  - "SPECIFIC_THEN_NO_RESERVATION": Prefer specific, fallback to on-demand.
  - "ANY_RESERVATION_THEN_FAIL": Consume any, fail if none.
  Possible values are: `NO_RESERVATION`, `ANY_RESERVATION`, `SPECIFIC_RESERVATION`, `SPECIFIC_THEN_ANY_RESERVATION`, `SPECIFIC_THEN_NO_RESERVATION`, `ANY_RESERVATION_THEN_FAIL`.

* `specific_reservation` -
  (Optional)
  Required when type is SPECIFIC_RESERVATION
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_zone_configuration_reservation_affinity_specific_reservation).


<a name="nested_zone_configuration_reservation_affinity_specific_reservation"></a>The `specific_reservation` block supports:

* `key` -
  (Optional)
  The key for the reservation. To target a SPECIFIC_RESERVATION by name,
  use "compute.googleapis.com/reservation-name".

* `values` -
  (Optional)
  The values for the reservation. Typically the name of the reservation.
  For shared reservations, use the format
  "projects/different-project/reservations/some-reservation-name".
  Limited to a single value.

<a name="nested_zone_configuration_node_affinities"></a>The `node_affinities` block supports:

* `key` -
  (Optional)
  The key for the node affinity label.

* `operator` -
  (Optional)
  Defines the operation of node selection.
  Possible values:
  - "IN": Requires Compute Engine to seek for matched nodes.
  - "NOT_IN": Requires Compute Engine to avoid certain nodes.
  Possible values are: `IN`, `NOT_IN`.

* `values` -
  (Optional)
  Corresponds to the label values of Node resource.

<a name="nested_networking_auto_configuration"></a>The `networking_auto_configuration` block supports:

* `internal` -
  (Optional)
  Internal networking configuration.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_networking_auto_configuration_internal).


<a name="nested_networking_auto_configuration_internal"></a>The `internal` block supports:

* `stack_type` -
  (Optional)
  Determine which IP addresses to automatically create.
  Possible values:
  - "IPV4_ONLY": Assign IPv4 address only.
  - "IPV4_IPV6": Assign both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.
  - "IPV6_ONLY": Assign IPv6 address only.
  Possible values are: `IPV4_ONLY`, `IPV4_IPV6`, `IPV6_ONLY`.

* `ip_address` -
  (Optional)
  IP addresses will be automatically allocated according to
  StackType if not provided.

* `ipv6_address` -
  (Optional)
  IP addresses will be automatically allocated according to
  StackType if not provided.

## Attributes Reference

In addition to the arguments listed above, the following computed attributes are exported:

* `id` - an identifier for the resource with format `projects/{{project}}/regions/{{region}}/haControllers/{{name}}`
* `self_link` - The URI of the created resource.


## Timeouts

This resource provides the following
[Timeouts](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/plugin/sdkv2/resources/retries-and-customizable-timeouts) configuration options:

- `create` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `delete` - Default is 20 minutes.

## Import


HaController can be imported using any of these accepted formats:

* `projects/{{project}}/regions/{{region}}/haControllers/{{name}}`
* `{{project}}/{{region}}/{{name}}`
* `{{region}}/{{name}}`
* `{{name}}`

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, use an [`identity` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/block/import#identity) to import HaController using identity values. For example:

```tf
import {
  identity = {
    name = "<-required value->"
    region = "<-required value->"
    project = "<-optional value->"
  }
  to = google_compute_ha_controller.default
}
```

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import HaController using one of the formats above. For example:

```tf
import {
  id = "projects/{{project}}/regions/{{region}}/haControllers/{{name}}"
  to = google_compute_ha_controller.default
}
```

When using the [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import), HaController can be imported using one of the formats above. For example:

```
$ terraform import google_compute_ha_controller.default projects/{{project}}/regions/{{region}}/haControllers/{{name}}
$ terraform import google_compute_ha_controller.default {{project}}/{{region}}/{{name}}
$ terraform import google_compute_ha_controller.default {{region}}/{{name}}
$ terraform import google_compute_ha_controller.default {{name}}
```

## User Project Overrides

This resource supports [User Project Overrides](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google/latest/docs/guides/provider_reference#user_project_override).
